knowledge_boundary: Only landing + prior_sections + active packet; zero Python outside packet.

exercises.code:
from jinja2 import Template
print(Template("Hola {{ nombre }}").render(nombre="Ana"))

from jinja2 import Template
print(Template("Lima median={{ median }} PEN (n={{ n }})").render(median=28, n=40))

median = None
print("—" if median is None else median)

from jinja2 import Template
rows = [{"region": "Lima", "value": 28}, {"region": "Cusco", "value": 22}]
print(Template("{% for r in rows %}{{ r.region }}:{{ r.value }}\n{% endfor %}").render(rows=rows).strip())

sections = [{"name": "Resumen", "h": 1}, {"name": "Método", "h": 1}]
print([s["name"] for s in sections])

sections = [{"h": 1}, {"h": 2}, {"h": 1}]
print(sum(1 for s in sections if s["h"] == 1))

chars, pages = 10, 2
print("needs_ocr" if chars / pages < 20 else "digital")

text = ""
print({"needs_ocr": not bool(text)})

hallazgo = {"id": "H1", "evidencia": "Tabla1"}
print(hallazgo["id"])

findings = [{"id": "H1"}, {"id": "H2"}]
print([f["id"] for f in findings])

dash, xlsx, doc = 28.0, 28.0, 28.0
print(dash == xlsx == doc)

cap = "Fig1 | Fuente: sintetico | n=10"
print("Fuente" in cap and "n=" in cap)

def check_parity(a, b, c):
    return a == b == c
print(check_parity(1, 1, 2))
print(check_parity(1, 1, 1))

vals = [28.04, 28.0]
print([round(v, 1) for v in vals])
print({"status": "pending_review"}["status"])

import hashlib
print(hashlib.sha1(b"synthetic").hexdigest()[:8])

selfcheck.justification_from_packet: Theory: separate data and Jinja template to reuse presentation and audit metrics in Python.
Theory: PDF nearly without text layer needs OCR/image treatment.
Theory: parity = same key metrics in dashboard, Excel and document.
Theory: CP-N2-B close includes provenance, visual checklist, traceable findings.
